zh.lifeSummary: 當Irmgard von Berg於1249年出生在德國時，她的父親Adolf IV. von Berg27歲，母親Marguerite de Hochstaden35歲。她於大約1273年與Eberhard I. von der Mark結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1294年3月22日在德國諾爾德海-維斯特法倫可萊斯 武納福洛登格 盧爾享年45歲，埋葬在德國諾爾德海-維斯特法倫可萊斯 武納福洛登格 盧爾。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Irmgard von Berg于1249年出生在德国时，她的父亲Adolf IV. von Berg27岁，母亲Marguerite de Hochstaden35岁。她于大约1273年与Eberhard I. von der Mark结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1294年3月22日在德国諾爾德海-維斯特法倫可萊斯 武納福洛登格 盧爾享年45岁，埋葬在德国諾爾德海-維斯特法倫可萊斯 武納福洛登格 盧爾。

nameMeanings.name: von
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Altered form of Welsh   Vaughan  .
Chinese: variant of   Wong  .
Cambodian: written វ៉ុន, unexplained.

variants.name: Vaughan
meanings: Welsh: from fychan, a lenited form of bychan, a diminutive of bach ‘little’. This was often used to distinguish the younger of two bearers of the same personal name, typically the son of a father with the same name.

nameMeanings.name: Berg
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik, Lennart, Bjorn, Lars, Nils, Alf, Nels, Thor, Tor, Knute, Selmer, Anders.

German and Dutch: topographic name for someone who lived on or by a hill or mountain, from Middle High German berc, Middle Dutch berghe. This surname is also found in some other parts of Europe, e.g. in France (Alsace and Lorraine) and Poland. Compare Dutch Vandenberg .
Norwegian and Danish: habitational name from a farmstead named with Old Norse bjarg ‘mountain, hill’.

variants.name: Berger
meanings: German, Dutch, Swedish, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): topographic name for someone who lived in the mountains or hills (see   Berg  ). The surname of German origin is also found in many other European countries, e.g. in France (Alsace and Lorraine), Russia, Poland, Czechia, Hungary, and Croatia, and Slovenia, often as a translation into German of corresponding Slavic topographic names or surnames. As a Jewish name it is mainly artificial. Compare   Bargar  ,   Barger  ,   Barker  ,   Barrier  , and   Barriger  .
French: occupational name from Old French bergier ‘shepherd’ (from Late Latin berbicarius, from berbex ‘ram’). It is also found in England, as a surname of Huguenot origin. Compare Shepard .
Norwegian: habitational name from any of various farms so named with the plural of   Berg   ‘mountain’.

variants.name: Bergh
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Johan, Anders, Arndt, Owe, Per, Sigvard, Sven, Syvert.
Scandinavian: variant of   Berg  .
Dutch and English: topographic name denoting someone living on or by a hill, Middle Dutch berghe, Middle English bergh ‘hill’ (Old English beorg). Compare Dutch Berg and Vandenbergh .

variants.name: Vandenberg
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Dutch Dirk, Marinus, Gerrit, Gerritt, Hendrik, Jacobus, Maarten, Pieter, Wim, Albertus, Durk, Hendrick.
Dutch (Van den Berg) and Flemish (also Van den Berg): topographic name for someone who lived on or by a hill, Middle Dutch berghe. Compare Berg .

variants.name: Berglin
meanings: Swedish: variant of Berg + the suffix -lin, derived from Latin -(l)inius ‘relating to’.

variants.name: Berget
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Knut.
Norwegian: habitational name from any of several farm names especially in eastern Norway named with the definite singular form of berg ‘mountain, hill’ (see Berg ).
French: topographic name, a derivative of   Berge   ‘river bank’.

variants.name: Baerg
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Gerhard, Kurt.
German (Bärg): variant of Berg .

variants.name: Bergen
meanings: German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): habitational name from any of various places called Bergen.
Dutch and Flemish: topographic name for someone who lived on or by a hill (see   Berg  ), or a habitational name from any of various places so named (see   Van Bergen  ).
Swedish (mainly Bergén): ornamental or habitational name formed with the (placename) element berg ‘mountain, hill’ + the adjectival suffix -en/-én, a derivative of Latin -enius ‘relating to’.

variants.name: Berghorst
meanings: Dutch (Gelderland): topographic name for someone living on or by a hill with shrubberies (see   Berg   and   Horst  ).

variants.name: Barg
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Bernhard, Erwin, Hans, Mathias, Wilfried.
North German:
topographic name for someone who lived on a hill, Low German barg. Compare Berg .

variants.name: Berge
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Maren, Johan, Karsten, Alf, Bent, Erik, Gudmund, Knud, Peer, Per, Sten, Thor.
Norwegian and Swedish: variant of   Berg  , from the dative form. As a Norwegian surname, this is a habitational name from any of numerous farms so named all over the country.
German: topographic name for a person by or on a hill, from an extended form of Berg (formerly with a preposition with dative case, e.g. am Berge).

nameMeanings.name: Irmgard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: from erm(en) ‘whole, entire’ + gard ‘enclosure’.

variants.name: Irmengard
meanings: Variant of   Ermengard .

variants.name: Irmingard
meanings: from erm(en) ‘whole, entire’ + gard ‘enclosure’.

variants.name: Ermgard
meanings: from erm(en) ‘whole, entire’ + gard ‘enclosure’.

en.lifeSummary: When Irmgard von Berg was born in 1249, in Germany, her father, Adolf IV. von Berg, was 27 and her mother, Marguerite de Hochstaden, was 35. She married Eberhard I. von der Mark about 1273. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 2 daughters. She died on 22 March 1294, in Fröndenberg Ruhr, Unna, North Rhine-Westphalia, Germany, at the age of 45, and was buried in Fröndenberg Ruhr, Unna, North Rhine-Westphalia, Germany.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Irmgard von Berg est née en 1249, à Allemagne, son père, Adolf IV. von Berg, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Marguerite de Hochstaden, avait 35 ans. Elle a épousé Eberhard I. von der Mark environ 1273. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée le 22 mars 1294, à Fröndenberg Ruhr, Kreis Unna, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Allemagne, à l’âge de 45 ans, et a été enterrée à Fröndenberg Ruhr, Kreis Unna, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Allemagne.

de.lifeSummary: Als Irmgard von Berg 1249 in Deutschland geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Adolf IV. von Berg 27 und ihre Mutter Marguerite de Hochstaden 35. Sie hat um 1273 Eberhard I. von der Mark geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb am 22. März 1294 im Alter von 45 Jahren in Fröndenberg Ruhr, Kreis Unna, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Deutschland und wurde in Fröndenberg Ruhr, Kreis Unna, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Deutschland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Irmgard von Berg è nata il nel 1249 nel Germania, sua padre, Adolf IV. von Berg, aveva 27 anni e sua madre, Marguerite de Hochstaden, aveva 35 anni. Lei sposato Eberhard I. von der Mark circa 1273. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 2 figlie. Morì il 22 marzo 1294, nel Fröndenberg Ruhr, Kreis Unna, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Germania, all'età di 45 anni, e fu sepolta a Fröndenberg Ruhr, Kreis Unna, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Germania.

ja.lifeSummary: Irmgard von Bergが1249年にドイツで生まれた時，父Adolf IV. von Bergは27歳，母Marguerite de Hochstadenは35歳であった。約1273年にEberhard I. von der Markと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1294年3月22日にドイツノードルヘイン-ウエストファレンクレイス ウナフルoンデンバーグ ルhルで45歳で死去し，ドイツノードルヘイン-ウエストファレンクレイス ウナフルoンデンバーグ ルhルにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Irmgard von Berg(이)가 1249년 독일에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Adolf IV. von Berg(은)는 27세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Marguerite de Hochstaden(은)는 35세였다. 그녀는 대략 1273년 Eberhard I. von der Mark(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1294년 3월 22일 독일 노르드하이-w스트파렌 크레이스 나 프o덴버그 루h르에서 45세의 나이로 사망했으며 독일 노르드하이-w스트파렌 크레이스 나 프o덴버그 루h르에 묻혔다.

Ela casou-se com Eberhard I. von der Mark aproximadamente 1273. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 22 de março de 1294, em Fröndenberg Ruhr, Kreis Unna, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Alemanha, com 45 anos, e foi sepultada em Fröndenberg Ruhr, Kreis Unna, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Alemanha.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Irmgard von Berg родилась в 1249 г. в местности Германия, её отцу по имени Adolf IV. von Berg было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Marguerite de Hochstaden, 35. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Eberhard I. von der Mark около 1273 г. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла 22 марта 1294 г. в местности фрoнденбирг рур, крис унна, нордраин-вестфеилен, Германия в возрасте 45 лет и была похоронена в местности фрoнденбирг рур, крис унна, нордраин-вестфеилен, Германия.

Murió el 22 de marzo de 1294, en Fröndenberg Ruhr, Kreis Unna, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Alemania, a la edad de 45 años, y fue sepultada en Fröndenberg Ruhr, Kreis Unna, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Alemania.
